results.title: Tamil Nadu: 10-year-old girl out on grocery errand kidnapped, killed; CM Vijay reacts
results.description: A 10-year-old girl was found dead in Coimbatore on Friday. The child was reportedlly out of her house to go to a nearby shop, when she was abducted and taken away on a bike. Her body was found near the Kannampalayam lake in Sulur area of the city. Two people have been arrested in the case so far.

results.title: Chinese-made weapons seized in Mumbai; three arms suppliers arrested
results.description: Mumbai, May 23 (IANS) In a major crackdown, the Anti-Extortion Cell of the Mumbai Crime Branch has arrested three alleged criminals who had reportedly arrived in the city to sell illegal foreign-made weapons, officials said on Saturday. results.title: Karnataka to get its first official state insect and frog after six-year wait
results.description: Karnataka government will soon declare Tudave Jenu honeybee as the state insect and the rare Malabar tree toad as the state frog to strengthen biodiversity conservation efforts. In a major step towards strengthening biodiversity conservation, the Karnataka government has announced that it will soon officially declare the Tudave Jenu honeybee species (Apis cerana) as the state insect and the Malabar tree toad (Pedostibes tuberculosus) as the state frog. The announcement was made by Forest and Ecology Minister M.B. Eshwar Khandre during the International Biodiversity Day programme held at Vidhana Soudha in Bengaluru. The formal government notification is expected to be issued within the next few days. The decision comes nearly six years after experts first recommended recognising the two species for their ecological importance and conservation value. Also Read: Bengaluru-Bidar flights return as Karnataka pumps ₹28.47 crore to revive air connectivity Speaking at the event, Khandre said several countries and states across the world identify specific species as symbols of biodiversity protection and environmental awareness. Karnataka has already declared sandalwood as the state tree, elephant as the state animal, Neelakantha or Indian roller as the state bird and Southern Birdwing butterfly (Troides minos) as the state butterfly. The addition of a state insect and state frog is now being seen as another important conservation initiative. The Karnataka Biodiversity Board had recommended the two species during 2020-21 after consultations with experts from multiple institutions, including entomologists from the University of Agricultural Sciences (UAS), ecologists from the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), batrachologists and officials from horticulture and home departments. Experts have expressed concern over the declining population of the Apis cerana honeybee species. Habitat destruction, climate change and the spread of the Thai sacbrood virus are said to be major threats affecting the species. The Malabar tree toad, meanwhile, is considered a rare amphibian species found in the Western Ghats. The species was first documented during 1875-76 but disappeared from records for more than 100 years before being rediscovered in Kerala in 1980. Later, the species was also found in parts of Karnataka’s Western Ghats region. Also Read: Even during heavy rain, these Bengaluru areas stay flood-free; Here’s why During the programme, Minister Khandre also raised concern over shrinking forest cover and increasing ecological pressure across the state. He stated that the government is intensifying efforts to reclaim forest land worth nearly ₹50,000 crore currently under the possession of the Indian Air Force (IAF), HMT and some private rubber plantation companies. According to the Minister, the government is currently fighting legal battles in different courts to recover the forest land and protect Karnataka’s ecological balance. The latest move to recognise these species is expected to create greater awareness about wildlife conservation and biodiversity protection in Karnataka.

results.title: Over 300 suspected child predators arrested in raids across California
results.description: Hundreds of suspected child predators have been apprehended and dozens of minors rescued in a sweeping multi-agency crackdown across Southern California
